methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $11M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Furno Materials is a seed-stage Climate & Energy company founded by Gurinder Nagra that has raised $11M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparative universe of 9 private peers and 1 public comparable (LNZA), including series-stage companies such as Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy, alongside a broader frontier-tech peer set including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $11M raised as a proxy for current value and applying a typical 2.5x seed-to-next-stage multiplier, implies a valuation of $28M. Peer-median analysis across three same-sector, adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $150M, with a range spanning $110M (Steady Energy) to $200M (Graphyte). The peer-median method reflects the higher risk-adjusted returns expected in early-stage climate technology relative to stage-progression mechanics alone.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $23M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $89M across both methods. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around Furno's position relative to its peer cohort and the stage-appropriate risk premium for seed-stage capital intensity in frontier energy. Refinement would require clarity on technology differentiation and capital requirements relative to the Series A comparables.
